summary.intent: Address all review comments on PR #541 in the moltis/mango-collision codebase and resolve the PR conversations.
summary.outcome: All 5 review comments were addressed: validation log level fixed, strip_markdown underscore bug fixed, truncate_at_char_boundary deduplicated, hardcoded Firecrawl URL replaced with constant, and fallback warning added. Changes committed and pushed.

repo: Firecrawl tooling is split across three files: firecrawl.rs (core), web_fetch.rs, and web_search.rs — all sharing the same base URL constant
truncate_at_char_boundary was duplicated between firecrawl.rs and web_fetch.rs; canonical version now lives in web_fetch.rs as pub(crate)
strip_markdown was incorrectly replacing underscores, which mangled URLs and identifiers — underscores must be preserved
Validation warnings for missing API keys should use tracing::info not warn level, and search.api_key must also be checked alongside firecrawl.api_key

code.finding: DEFAULT_BASE_URL constant made pub(crate) so web_fetch.rs and web_search.rs can reference it instead of hardcoding the string
truncate_at_char_boundary marked pub(crate) here; firecrawl.rs now imports and reuses it instead of duplicating
Added tracing::warn! when firecrawl fallback is enabled but no API key is configured, preventing silent degradation
New test added to assert underscores are preserved through strip_markdown; 22 firecrawl tests total after this session

workflow: Format + clippy + full test suite run before committing review fixes is standard practice in this repo
641 tests pass in the tools crate; test count is a useful sanity check after refactors

friction: PR review comments required reading raw tool output files from a previous session's cache path, adding an extra extraction step
open_items: PR conversations were addressed in code but the transcript does not confirm they were resolved on GitHub — may need manual resolution or a gh API call to mark them resolved
